item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't do much for me. The second I read that it went by Ian, I could guess the contours of the article, and was not disappointed. The log was what actually led me to downvote, as it's basically just "yeah, Ian was a person," but much too long.

The rest of the article felt pretty perfunctory, to the extent that I began to wonder why Anderson needed to juice their PC cleaning software with THE HEART OF A FORESAKEN CHILD the soul of a OSU undergrad.

I think that this would have would much better had it focused on the whole "capitalism creates a Rube Goldberg machine of human suffering for a relatively mundane task" aspect of this. I feel like maybe that's what the final letter was grasping at, but there wasn't any tension, no hint of unease at the fact that they've made Clippy, but with a soul and he dies every time you unplug it. Instead, they just plough through with the most bland language imaginable ("this project," "the product," "these things," etc.)1.

Overall, no real surprises and not strong enough on the writing to avoid a downvote from me.

Footnotes
1. Upon re-reading, referring to Ian as "him" and "this project" is pretty stone-cold, but still feels flat to me.

item.title: Re: How was Ian "converted"
item.content:encoded:
I’m glad you managed to fix most of those memory problems you were having with Ian. Fingers crossed you’ll get the discharge problem under your thumb soon. Phineas's notes say there are some incantations we can try that will bind him to accept all commands and limit his functionality outside the desired medium, but that means we’re going to need a vessel much larger than a flash drive, which defeats the nimble design of the product. Just remember to keep this project quiet until I figure out how to get Vincent and Phineas on board.

If I am correct these things are going to sell like hotcakes. I’ve already taken the liberty of contacting a supplier Marshall recommended and stockpiling about twenty more of the raw material for when we hammer all the bugs out so we can get a jump on the demand.

Vincent Anderson actually didn't know that this was being made. Isaac acquired this raw material from Mr. Marshall, of a very infamous Ltd. of some renown.

item.title: How was Ian "converted"
item.content:encoded:
I wonder how Anderson put Ian's consciousness into a hard drive, because that's creepy as hell. I imagine that Anderson either abducted Ian or he was a employee of Anderson Robotics that didn't know what he was getting into.

Edit: I re-read it and he died from a car crash (most likely anyways) and then Anderson probably found his corpse and did… something to it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I would worry about if I had the choice of uploading myself. Could I get trapped doing someone's bidding? +1 exciting to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god…
I read the entire article, knowing that it is real person right after the date of creation glitch.
After I finished reading I was looking for timestamps to create the timeline of his creation.
It was at that time I realized the file names were fucking dates!
That guy remembers his second birthday!!!!
Or remembered it…
+1 from me… this thing hit right to the feels…

item.title: Image Update
item.content:encoded:
In what is quickly becoming The Great Anderson Robotics Reimaging of 2019, the original image for SCP-2306 was found to be non-compliant, and as a result, has been removed and replaced.

The new image was provided by Rounderhouse.

Source: Here

License: Public Domain

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A+

(Also, not sure why you posted that SCP draft here, in this thread, but you do you I suppose.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good one! I liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Started reading this and thinking it was just another AI skip. Then, when I reached the dog memory, it dreadfully dawned upon me that it was an actual human in there, enslaved and robbed of his dearest memories. Made me feel really uneasy reading on, and so incredibly sorry for the poor soul.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad to hear you liked the article.

If you haven't yet, I strongly encourage you to check out the other things written for Anderson Robotics, which can be found here, as they will likewise be up your alley. Maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending logs are what really sell this Skip for me. My favorite part about it, and about many of the skips on this site, are the implied and unanswered story elements that force the reader to interpret / fill in those gaps with their own creativity which is an effective tool for unsettling storytelling! This was a very enjoyable read and I'm excited to see what else you have up your sleeve in the future!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot damn!

But seriously, why are you around all those cut onions, seagull?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh? Is that a tear? It can't be, I just must have some, water on my face.

Easy +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are correct. Anderson would not appreciate Ian's existence at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a feeling if Anderson knew about this, he wouldn't like it very much..

Man, this entry gives me emotions. I feel so bad for the AI trapped in there, wanting to talk and feel, but being forced to forget and frozen up when he tries.

item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded: I loved this one!! Great post! One of my favorite ones!

item.title: As seen in Confinement...
item.content:encoded:
The new episode of the animated series "Confinement" has dropped, and a certain lost soul is one of the SCPs featured therein.

Check it out HERE.

10/10 would watch again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding the robocop vibe, which I did not expect but once I saw articulated here realized it fit perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing that the stepford smile aspect really makes this stand out among other AI/human soul in a machine articles.

item.title: Re: I am confuse
item.content:encoded:
What are the Andreson "skips," are they a tale classification, or other designation?

"Skip" ( or "Scip") is slang for "SCP article" or "SCP object" depending on on context.

The SCPs tagged "anderson" are.
SCP-1360, SCP-2306, SCP-2522, SCP-2806, SCP-2906 and SCP-2987.

You can generally answer questions like this using tag search.

For everything Anderson, you might want to check out the Anderson Robotics Hub.

item.title: I am confuse
item.content:encoded:
What are the Andreson "skips," are they a tale classification, or other designation? I have seen the term a from time to time, especially in this article discussion, but I am unclear as to its meaning.

Also: Mr. Conwell, do you have already or even want a picture for Ian's avatar? If you don't have one, but want one, then I can make a few jabs at using Microsoft Paint to make one. If you don't want one then that is okay, even logical in a way.

I hate to necropost, so I am sorry if this was annoying or unwanted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The "trapped in a typewriter" theme is mentioned above by djkaktus, but this distinctly reminds me of RoboCop more than anything else. In that sense it feels unique - I don't think another SCP has had me thinking of RoboCop of all things, or some dystopian flash drive startup with soylent green as it's secret ingredient.

Was an easy +1 for me when I got to the end, despite the main body of it feeling slightly trite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do aim to please. Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta say this one hit me hard in the emotion department. I can only say that for a handful of the pages and tales I have read.

Having the files left on the computer go through his life really struck me for whatever reason.

Excellent work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Appropriate edit has been made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just something to add to this list,

 and printing messages from a LaserJet printer.

There is actually no such thing as a "LaserJet". It should be either "Laser" (Black and white) or "InkJet" (Color). One of the less important fixes, but the less mistakes, the better!

item.title: A nifty YouTube video
item.content:encoded:
YouTube user TheLivingKnightmare has done a reading of the interview from this article. While its not exactly what I had in mind when I thought of Ian's voice (probably not lurkd had in mind when creating Dr. Bozkurt as well) its still a really neat reading.

Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h0oitA8gydo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to the Object Classes page, objects given the designation of Thaumiel are, "are utilized by the Foundation to contain or counteract the effects of other highly dangerous anomalies." Basically using one less dangerous anomaly to contain one more dangerous.

While it is true that Ian is quite helpful, you really can't use it to contain another anomaly (outside of something infecting a computer maybe). As such, Thaumiel just wouldn't really fly here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry if this comes out a a little bit basic, and I know only a few have the designation, but due the the "helpful" nature of this SCP wouldn't the class technically qualify as Thaumiel? Or does the term only apply to Anti-Keters?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thats funny, because when I was shopping the draft, those files were a part of the article that were either really liked, or really disliked. Glad to see you all fell into the first category.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much my thoughts. I was going to downvote until I read the files it left on the computer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see that Anderson is beginning to get some more traction as a GoI, however minor a GoI it maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm starting to get really excited about Anderson skips.

With this one, I'm not too sure about the cross-link in the image text, but you hooked me with the Foundation compositing an avatar for Ian. Great backstory on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the way parts of the images are selectively blurred out — he remembers (or did) the face of his prom date, but not the face of his driving instructor for instance.

The question my mind keeps going back to is: what is he experiencing when he freezes temporarily and is "corrected"? (shudder)

I also wonder whether the folder is the {something} purging his memory of "unnecessary" bits, or if it's him managing a tiny rebellion in offloading happy memories to the computer where the {something} (hopefully) can't find and remove them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, damn, this is pretty horrifying when you think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole "trapped in the typewriter" theme has been long since played out, but I haven't been able to help but feel that this approaches it in a way that's just off-putting enough to overlook any cliches. The fact that Ian is just so goddamn polite and customer service oriented, all while you just know he's a human soul, sets this pretty comfortably in the uncanny valley. What, then, is he actually expressing when he "lightens up"? Is he actually relaxed and aware of his situation? Or is he just parroting things he's been told like a machine?

Either way, the creep factor is real.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixes have been made.

Thanks for the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, just some grammar/phrasing stuff.

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-2306-1 will begin to run, regardless as to the operating system used by the computer

Should be "regardless of."

the presence of a microphone and camera on the computer allows for instance to both hear and see its surroundings

Should be "allows for the instance" or "an instance."

with SCP-2306-1 communicating via means available within the alternative mediums, including:

This feels awkward and a little redundant. It would obviously be communicating via available means, since it can't communicate via unavailable means, and "within the alternative mediums" is something we can assume. I would suggest "with SCP-2306 communicating through other means, including: [list]."

Do you have question regarding my functions?

Should be "questions" or "a question."

Dr. Bozkurt attempts at regaining operation of SCP-2306-1 fail.

Should be "Dr. Bozkurt's attempts."

Attempts to find a human match for this identity, either living or dead are currently ongoing.

Needs another comma after "dead."

an individual playing fetch with a dog (Black Lab)

"Black Lab" is informal; should probably write out "Labrador."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it, but there are a number of grammatical errors and awkward phrasings that need to be corrected.

A very close +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "stockpiling about twenty more of the raw material"… shudder

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
Its been a while since I've posted something related to Anderson, so why not another skip.

Special thanks to TwistedGears, Decibelles, djkaktus, Crayne, Dr_Leonard, minmin, and Randomini for looking at this back when it was a draft.

Enjoy the pie.

JC
